data.title: Top Midday Gainers
data.description:
Streamline Health Solutions shares soared 133% amid heavy intraday trading after MDaudit said Thursday it will acquire Streamline Health in an all-cash deal valued at about $37.4 million, including debt.

Over 2.6 million shares of Streamline traded intraday versus a daily average of roughly 8,000.

Zeo Energy said Thursday it has agreed to acquire Heliogen in an all-stock transaction valued at about $10 million.

Shares of Zeo soared 91%, with intraday trading volume catapulting to over 95.8 million compared with a daily average of roughly 400,000.

Alzamend Neuro said Thursday it has dosed the first patient of its first phase 2 clinical study of AL001, which aims to deliver lithium more effectively to patients with Alzheimer's, bipolar, major depressive, and post-traumatic disorders.

Shares surged 85% as intraday trading volume advanced to more than 73.1 million from a daily average of about 131,000.

data.title: Portugal's president invites caretaker PM to lead new government
data.description:
Portugal's president named Luis Montenegro, head of the centre-right Democratic Alliance (AD) that won a snap national election on May 18, as the prime minister on Thursday and invited him to form his second minority government, the presidency said.

The AD won 91 seats in the 230-seat parliament - 11 more than in the previous legislature, but still far from a working majority - in an election marked by far-right party Chega replacing the Socialist Party (PS) as the main opposition force.

Montenegro, who first came to power in March 2024, has refused to make any deals with the anti-establishment, anti-immigration Chega, which garnered 60 seats, two more than the PS after the latter's worst showing in four decades.

The office of President Marcelo Rebelo de Sousa said in a statement after he consulted the three main parties on Thursday that Montenegro would announce his new government after parliament convenes for its first session.

No confirmation vote is required, although any party can present a motion of rejection.

The election, the third in as many years, was called after Montenegro failed to win a vote of confidence in March when the opposition questioned his integrity over dealings of his family's consultancy firm.

He has denied any wrongdoing and voters ended up punishing the PS for its role in bringing down Montenegro's government, in what many people saw as an unnecessary election.

Parliament is expected to enable the new government on its return next week. PS's interim leader, Carlos Cesar, promised his support in that initial step.

While the government's first big test is likely to be the 2026 budget towards the end of the year, no new parliamentary poll can be called until at least mid-2026 because Portugal will hold a presidential election next January.

Although that doesn't guarantee political stability, the centre-left PS's weakened position is likely to make them a more amenable opposition with more affinities with the AD than with Chega, analysts say, seeing little danger of a legislative paralysis in the medium term.

data.title: Focusing on Fat and Flavor Could Raise Pork's Appeal — Market Talk
data.description: A new focus on fat and flavor could help boost domestic demand for pork in the U.S., in the wake of trade policy and global market dynamics that threaten the pace of export sales, CoBank says. While pork is the most widely consumed meat in the world, pork consumption in the U.S. has been flat for over 50 years, according to CoBank. The pork industry's new consumer marketing program is focusing on flavor and new cooking techniques as it encourages U.S. consumers to reimagine the use of pork for at-home meals, CoBank says. "A refreshed approach to hog genetics that focuses on fat content, flavor and consumer preferences over production efficiencies may be necessary to meaningfully grow domestic demand," CoBank adds. CoBank is a cooperative bank serving industries across rural America. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

data.title: Update: Exxon Mobil Optimistic About Winning Arbitration Case in Chevron-Hess Deal
data.description:
(Updates with statement from Hess in fourth paragraph.)

Exxon Mobil is optimistic about the outcome of the arbitration case relating to its contractual dispute in the Chevron's planned acquisition of Hess , an Exxon executive speaking at a Bernstein conference said Thursday, according to a FactSet transcript.

Neil Chapman, a senior vice president at Exxon, said he was confident that the arbitration panel would rule in favor of the company and determine that it had a right of first refusal over Hess's stake, the transcript showed.

There will be "no change for us" if Exxon loses, Chapman said, according to the transcript.

"We remain confident that the arbitration will confirm the Stabroek right of first refusal does not apply to the merger," a Hess spokesperson said in an emailed statement to MT Newswires. Hess owns a 30% stake in the Stabroek Block offshore Guyana, which Exxon operates.

Chevron did not immediately reply to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: Sector Update: Financial Stocks Advance Thursday Afternoon
data.description:
Financial stocks were rising in Thursday afternoon trading, with the NYSE Financial Index and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) each adding 0.2%.

The Philadelphia Housing Index rose 0.5%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE) increased 0.6%.

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) was declining 1% to $106,464, and the yield for 10-year US Treasuries was dropping 5.3 basis points to 4.43%.

In economic news, a US federal court ruling challenging a series of tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump likely weakens Washington's position in trade talks, but the administration has other tools to push through the tariffs agenda, analysts said Thursday. The Court of International Trade ruled Wednesday that Trump overstepped his authority by imposing duties under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The Trump administration has appealed the ruling, according to media reports.

US initial jobless claims rose to a level of 240,000 in the week ended May 24 from a downwardly revised 226,000 level in the previous week, compared with expectations for a smaller increase to 230,000 in survey of analysts compiled by Bloomberg.

US economic growth, measured by gross domestic product, was revised up to a 0.2% decrease in Q1 from a 0.3% drop in the advance estimate, above the expectations for no revision in a survey compiled by Bloomberg. GDP rose by 2.4% in Q4.

In corporate news, JPMorgan Chase named former HSBC dealmaker Kamal Jabre as new M&A vice chair to drive growth in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Reuters reported Thursday. JPMorgan shares added 0.1%, and HSBC rose 0.8%.

Former Goldman Sachs banker Tim Leissner was sentenced to two years in prison after pleading guilty to his role in looting the Malaysian sovereign investment fund 1MDB, news outlets reported Thursday. Goldman shares were down 0.8%.

Banco Santander is considering to start offering retail crypto services to customers of its digital bank and has applied for a license, Bloomberg reported. Santander shares added 0.3%.

data.title: Nucor Is Maintained at Buy by UBS
Onyx Gold Closes a $3-Million Financing Investment by Strategic Investor; Shares Down 8%
data.description:
Onyx Gold on Thursday said it closed the first tranche of a non-brokered private placement with a strategic investor for gross proceeds of about $3 million.

The company issued the investor 3.49-million shares at a price of $0.85 each.

Onyx will use proceeds of the first tranche of the private placement for general corporate purposes.

The company a second tranche of the private placement is scheduled to close on or about June 6.

The company's shares were last seen down $0.11 to $1.22 on the TSX Venture Exchange.

data.title: Sector Update: Energy Stocks Rise Thursday Afternoon
data.description:
Energy stocks rose Thursday afternoon with the NYSE Energy Sector Index up 0.1% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) adding 0.3%.

The Philadelphia Oil Service Sector index rose 0.4%, and the Dow Jones US Utilities index advanced 0.6%.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil fell 1.6% to $60.88 a barrel, and global benchmark Brent dropped 1.3% to $64.04 a barrel.

US natural gas stocks rose by 101 billion cubic feet in the week ended Friday, above the 100 billion increase expected in a survey compiled by Bloomberg and following a gain of 120 billion cubic feet in the previous week.

Henry Hub natural gas futures fell 2% to $3.49 per 1 million BTU.

In sector news, Exxon Mobil and a number of other major oil and gas companies are facing a climate-change lawsuit claiming wrongful death of an individual who died of hyperthermia, or overheating, the New York Times reported Thursday.

In corporate news, Chevron is planning to lay off 800 workers in Texas, effective July 15, as part of a global workforce reduction, Reuters reported, citing a notice to the Texas Workforce Commission. Chevron shares rose 1%.

Exxon Mobil expressed confidence in the outcome of the arbitration case regarding its contractual dispute over Chevron's planned acquisition of Hess , media outlets reported Thursday, citing an Exxon executive. Exxon shares added 0.3%.

Zeo Energy agreed to buy Heliogen for $10 million in stock. Zeo Energy shares surged 91%.

Matador Resources and its affiliate San Mateo Midstream announced the startup of the expansion of San Mateo's Marlan cryogenic natural gas processing plant in New Mexico. Matador shares gained 0.2%.

data.title: Powell Met With Trump to Discuss Economy, Interest Rates — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nicole Goodkind

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell met with President Donald Trump at the White House on Thursday, the central bank said, weeks after Powell told reporters he had not been invited back since Trump's return to office.

According to the Fed, Powell "met with the President today at the White House to discuss economic developments including for growth, employment, and inflation."

The meeting was held "at the President's invitation."

The Fed said Powell "did not discuss his expectations for monetary policy," but reiterated that rate decisions will be based on "careful, objective, and non-political analysis."

He also "stressed" that the Federal Open Market Committee's "path of policy will depend entirely on incoming economic information and what that means for the outlook."

The meeting comes amid escalating tension between the Fed and the White House. Trump has publicly blamed Powell for being too slow to cut interest rates, said his " termination cannot come fast enough," and " studied" whether he could remove him. Powell and other Fed officials have defended the central bank's independence.

The Fed's next policy meeting is scheduled for mid-June.

Write to Nicole Goodkind at nicole.goodkind@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Vuzix, Ramblr Collaborate to Support Development of AI-Powered Smart Glasses
data.description:
Vuzix said Thursday it is collaborating with German AI startup Ramblr to support the functionality of its smart glasses with advanced AI features for enterprise users.

The partnership aims to deliver real-time, context-aware guidance through smart glasses, allowing workers to access instructions instantly while performing tasks, the company said.

This integration allows the smart glasses to deliver visual and spoken instructions based on what the user sees, supporting automate complex tasks, it added.

Shares of the company were up over 12% in recent trading.

data.title: T2 Metals Provides Update on Fire Situation at Sherridon VMS Project, Manitoba
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - May 29, 2025) - T2 Metals Corp. (OTCQB: TWOSF) (WKN: A2DR6E) ("T2" or the "Company") provides an update regarding the wildfire situation in the vicinity of its Sherridon copper-gold-zinc Project located near Flin Flon, Manitoba.

The Company is actively monitoring the evolving wildfire situation in the region. Recent reports from the Manitoba Wildfire Service indicate that there are active fires very near the community of Sherridon and the Company's mineral concessions. The safety of our personnel, contractors, and the local community is T2 Metals' paramount concern.

As a precautionary measure and in adherence to community evacuation measures, T2 Metals has temporarily suspended field activities at its Sherridon project site. This measure enables continuous monitoring of the situation and ensures all personnel are safe and accounted for. The Company has clear communication channels with the Manitoba Wildfire Service and local authorities to receive real-time updates and guidance.

Mark Saxon, CEO of T2 Metals Corp. said, "Our thoughts are with the community of Sherridon, the Kiciwapa Cree Nation and other individuals affected by the wildfires across Manitoba and Saskatchewan. The safety of our team and the residents of Sherridon is our top priority and we remain in close contact with the community. Field operations will resume as soon as it is safe and advisable to do so. We anticipate minimal long-term impact on our exploration schedule or loss of property, given the proactive measures taken."

About T2 Metals Corp (TSX.V: TWO) (OTCQB: TWOSF) (WKN: A2DR6E)

T2 Metals Corp is an emerging copper and precious metal company enhancing shareholder value through exploration and discovery. The Company continues to target under-explored areas, including the Sherridon, Lida, Cora and Copper Eagle projects where post-mineralization cover masks areas of high geological prospectivity in the vicinity of major mines.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD, 

  • "Mark Saxon" Mark SaxonPresident & CEO
  • For further information, please contact: t2metals.com 1305 - 1090 West Georgia St., Vancouver, BC, V6E 3V7info@t2metals.com

data.title: Meta Platforms Teams Up With Anduril to Develop Extended Reality Products for US Military
data.description:
Meta Platforms is partnering with American defense technology company Anduril to design and build extended reality products for the US military, according to a joint statement Thursday.

The companies said they will work together to provide warfighters with products that enhance perception and enable intuitive control of autonomous platforms on the battlefield. They said the collaboration builds on the social media company's existing Reality Labs investments and its efforts to boost the adoption of the Llama AI models for national security purposes.

Meta and Anduril said the partnership is funded through private capital and without taxpayer money and is designed to save the US military "billions of dollars."

data.title: SentinelOne Is Maintained at Neutral by UBS
Erdogan says Russia's proposal for Istanbul talks heightens hope for peace
data.description:
Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan said Russia's proposal to hold another round of peace talks with Ukraine in Istanbul on June 2 had heightened Ankara's hopes for peace.

NATO member Turkey has maintained good relations with both sides since the start of the war. It hosted an initial round of peace talks in March 2022 - a month after Russia's full-scale invasion of its neighbour - and again on May 16, the first direct meeting between Moscow and Kyiv in three years.

Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan held talks in Moscow on Monday and Tuesday, including with Russian President Vladimir Putin. He will also meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy and other Ukrainian officials in Kyiv on Friday.

Speaking to reporters on a flight from Azerbaijan, Erdogan said Ankara was in contact with both countries, and added that the recent momentum in peace efforts was an opportunity to reach lasting peace.

"The road to a resolution goes through more dialogue, more diplomacy. We are using all our diplomatic power and potential for peace," he was cited as saying by his office on Thursday.

Russia is under increasing pressure to agree a ceasefire, and while delegates from Moscow and Kyiv did not agree on one in Istanbul this month, they agreed to trade 1,000 prisoners of war and share their views on the contours of a truce.

Fidan, speaking to Turkish media representatives on a train ride from Poland to Kyiv, said that while the sides' demands for a ceasefire were different, they needed to converge their views to reach an agreement, adding other actors needed to help too.

"The Russians have goals regarding the war, of course. These are goals they set themselves. They have targets regarding land, regarding the annexation of certain areas. How acceptable these are for the international community, in terms of international law, that is all debatable," he said.

"I don't really evaluate that this will be tied to a legal process too much," he added, saying he could call U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio following his talks in Kyiv.

data.title: Iqstel to Buy Majority Stake in Fintech Company GlobeTopper
data.description:
By Chris Wack

Iqstel said it is buying 51% of fintech company GlobeTopper in a deal valued at $700,000, following a March 21 memo of understanding.

GlobeTopper specializes in advanced B2B Top-Up solutions, and has operations across America, Europe and Africa.

The telecommunications company said the transaction becomes effective July 1.

Iqstel shares were down 9% to $8.25 in afternoon trading.

As part of the transaction, Craig Span will continue in his role as chief executive of GlobeTopper.

The transaction includes $200,000 in staged cash payments, and $500,000 in restricted Iqstel common, along with certain milestone payments.

The company plans to invest up to $1.2 million over the next two years in GlobeTopper, contingent on GlobeTopper meeting specific quarterly financial targets.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: Dick's Sporting Goods Is Maintained at Buy by UBS
Weekly Jobless Claims Rise, Continuing Applications Hit Highest Since November 2021
data.description:
Weekly applications for unemployment insurance jumped more than projected, while continuing claims climbed to the highest reading since November 2021, US government data showed Thursday.

The seasonally adjusted number of initial claims increased to 240,000 in the week ended May 24 from the previous week's downwardly revised reading of 226,000, according to the Department of Labor. The Bloomberg-polled consensus was for an increase to 230,000 from the prior week's original 227,000 level.

The four-week moving average totaled 230,750, dropping by 250 from the prior week's average, which was revised lower. Unadjusted claims rose by 10,742 on a weekly basis to 212,506.

For the week ended May 17, the seasonally adjusted continuing claims totaled about 1.92 million, the highest level since Nov. 13, 2021. Wall Street's views were for a 1.89 million reading. Continuing claims increased by 26,000 from the previous week's downwardly revised level. The four-week moving average rose by 2,750 at 1.89 million, the highest since Nov. 27, 2021, according to the report.

"The labor market still appears to be in good shape," Jefferies Chief US Economist Thomas Simons wrote in a note. "Some slowdown in leisure and hospitality hiring may manifest in the payroll data (to be) released next week, but any such slowdown is far more likely to be driven by weather than tariffs."

Describing this month's weather as "terrible," Simons said that it has rained significantly more than average across the East Coast and throughout the South.

The US Court of International Trade on Wednesday ruled that President Donald Trump overstepped his authority by imposing tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The Trump administration has appealed the ruling, according to media reports.

Minutes from the Federal Reserve's most recent monetary policy meeting released Wednesday showed that policymakers were concerned about the economic impact of tariffs.

data.title: Rio2 Virtual AGM Reminder
data.description:
VANCOUVER, British Columbia, May 29, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Rio2 Limited (“Rio2” or the “Company”) (TSXV: RIO; OTCQX: RIOFF; BVL: RIO) wishes to remind its shareholders that its forthcoming Annual General and Special Meeting of shareholders (the “Meeting” or the “AGM”) will be held on June 4, 2025, at 11 a.m. ET. This will be an entirely virtual Meeting to be conducted via live webcast, where all shareholders, regardless of geographic location, will have an equal opportunity to participate online. Shareholders will not be able to attend the Meeting in person.

The timing and process for voting are described in detail in the April 17, 2025, Notice of Availability of Proxy Materials mailed to shareholders and meeting materials available on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca and the Company’s website at www.rio2.com/investors.

VOTING DEADLINE

The voting deadline is 11:00 a.m. (ET) on Monday, June 2, 2025.

PARTICIPATION AT THE MEETING

Registered Shareholders and duly appointed proxyholders can participate in the Meeting in real-time online at www.meetnow.global/MPTTPVV by clicking “Shareholder” and entering the 15-digit Control Number located on the form of proxy. Duly appointed proxyholders can participate in the Meeting by entering an invitation code provided by Computershare before the start of the Meeting.

Voting at the Meeting will only be available for Registered Shareholders and duly appointed proxyholders. Non-registered shareholders who have not appointed themselves may attend the Meeting by clicking “Guest” and completing the online form.

Rio2 recommends that shareholders log in to the webcast at least 10 minutes prior to the virtual Meeting start time to ensure connectivity.

Shareholders with questions about voting their shares or attending the Annual General Meeting (AGM) may contact the Company by email at info@rio2.com.

Your vote is important. Whether or not you plan to attend the Meeting virtually, please vote as soon as possible by one of the methods described in the proxy materials for the AGM to ensure that your shares are represented and voted at the AGM.

ABOUT RIO2 LIMITED

Rio2 is a mining company with a focus on development and mining operations with a team that has proven technical skills as well as successful capital markets track record. Rio2 is focused on taking its Fenix Gold Project in Chile to production in the shortest possible timeframe based on a staged development strategy. Rio2 and its wholly owned subsidiary, Fenix Gold Limitada, are companies with the highest environmental standards and responsibility with the firm conviction that it is possible to develop mining projects that respect the three pillars (Social, Environment, Economics) of responsible development. As related companies, we reaffirm our commitment to apply environmental standards beyond those that are mandated by regulators, seeking to protect and preserve the environment of the territories that we operate in.

To learn more about Rio2 Limited, please visit: www.rio2.com or Rio2's SEDAR+ profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD OF RIO2 LIMITED

Alex Black

Executive Chairman

Tel: +51 99279 4655

Executive Vice President, CFO & Corporate Secretary

data.title: Trump Told Powell He's Making a Mistake on Rates — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

The Federal Reserve said that Chair Jerome Powell met with President Trump at the White House on Thursday "to discuss economic developments including for growth, employment and inflation."

The meeting was at Trump's invitation, the Fed said.

Powell didn't discuss his specific expectations for monetary policy, the Fed said. He did stress that policy decisions - i.e., the path of interest rates - will depend on economic data.

On May 7, Powell was asked at a press event why he hadn't met with Trump this year. "I've never asked for a meeting with any president and I never will," Powell said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Synopsys Confirms China Export Ban. The Stock Drops. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Synopsys stock was falling on Thursday after the semiconductor software design company confirmed that it had received notice from the Bureau of Industry and Security of the U.S. Commerce Department regarding new export restrictions related to China.

The company confirmed that it received the letter from BIS one day after the Financial Times reported Wednesday afternoon that the Commerce Department told electronic design automation companies like Cadence Design Systems and Synopsys to stop supplying their technology to China. Synopsys said on its second-quarter earnings call Wednesday night that it hadn't received notice from BIS, and reiterated its financial guidance for the full year.

Synopsys said Thursday that it is now suspending its financial guidance.

"Synopsys is currently assessing the potential impact of the BIS Letter on its business, operating results and financial condition," the company said in a news release.

Shares of Synopsys were down 4% Thursday afternoon. Rival Cadence was down 2.2%. Cadence didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
